summary_oneline: Port flowtask's CompanyScraper into an ai-parrot CompanyResearch toolkit: agent sends a company name, gets a structured CompanyProfile scraped from LeadIQ/RocketReach/SICCode/VisualVisitor/ZoomInfo/Explorium

localization.role: source component to port: search→validate→fetch→parse pipeline
per-source extractor contract to re-model in parrot
existing parrot mixin providing _get, _search_duckduckgo, _search_google, proxy rotation — the reuse target
base class the new toolkit inherits; auto tool generation from async methods
house-style exemplar for args schema + tool docs

constraints.statement: Parrot already ships HTTPService with DuckDuckGo/Google-CSE search and proxylists rotation; the port must reuse it instead of copying flowtask interfaces

No SeleniumService exists in parrot; ZoomInfo's undetected-chromedriver get() and the Selenium Google-CSE fallback cannot be ported as-is (selenium/undetected-chromedriver ARE available in the 'agents' extra)
fuzzywuzzy is not a parrot dependency; company-name validation needs rapidfuzz/thefuzz or difflib
DataFrame/tqdm batch semantics are flowtask-specific and must be dropped for a per-company async tool call; structured output should be a Pydantic model per CLAUDE.md
VisualVisitor parser is a copy of RocketReach's and mislabels source_platform='rocketreach' — fix during port
No existing company-research code in parrot/tools — greenfield placement, no collisions with recent tools/ work

whats_new: parrot/tools/company_research/ package: CompanyResearchToolkit(AbstractToolkit) exposing a research_company(company_name, sources?) tool

CompanyProfile Pydantic model (name, website, address fields, phone, stock_symbol, naics/sic codes, employee_count, revenue_range, industry, description, similar_companies, executives, source_platform, status)
Extractor base (CompanyExtractor) + six extractors ported from flowtask parsers (LeadIQ, RocketReach, SICCode, VisualVisitor, ZoomInfo, Explorium)
Unit tests with fixture HTML pages per source (no live scraping in CI)
non_goals: No DataFrame/batch API (flowtask keeps that role)
No paid API integrations (RocketReach/ZoomInfo official APIs) — this ports the public-page scraping approach
No changes to parrot/interfaces/http.py beyond consuming it

patterns_to_follow.pattern: AbstractToolkit auto-tool generation + tool_prefix, docstring-driven descriptions
search→title-keyword validation→fuzzy name check→fetch→parse pipeline from CompanyScraper._search_company

integration_risks.risk: Anti-bot walls (ZoomInfo especially) make live scraping flaky without Selenium/undetected-chrome; DDG rate limits already handled via RatelimitException in flowtask
Site markup drift: all six parsers are selector-coupled to current HTML; tests must use recorded fixtures

unknowns.question: Aggregation strategy: should research_company return the FIRST successful source (flowtask behavior) or query ALL requested sources and merge into one profile with per-field provenance?
plausible_answers: a) first-success in priority order (fast, flowtask parity)
b) query all sources and merge (richer, slower)
c) parameter: sources list + merge flag, default first-success
unknowns.user_answer: First-success in priority order (flowtask parity); sources param still lets callers pick

unknowns.question: ZoomInfo requires undetected-chromedriver (no SeleniumService in parrot). Ship ZoomInfo in v1 behind the 'agents' extra with a minimal Selenium helper, port it to Playwright, or defer ZoomInfo to a follow-up?
plausible_answers: b) minimal undetected-chromedriver helper inside the toolkit, optional import
unknowns.user_answer: Playwright fetcher — port the browser path to playwright (already in the agents extra); ZoomInfo stays in v1 scope

unknowns.question: Tool surface: one aggregate tool (research_company) only, or also expose per-source tools (company_from_rocketreach, etc.) for the LLM to pick?
plausible_answers: a) single research_company tool with optional sources param

confidence_map.claim: Flowtask pipeline is search(DDG→Google CSE)→validate title→fetch page→BS4 parse, per source
Per-source contract is ScrapperBase: domain, site: search template, keywords, define_search_term(), async scrapping()
parrot HTTPService already provides _get/_search_duckduckgo/_search_google/proxies — port reuses it
AbstractToolkit turns public async methods into tools; new toolkit follows OpenAPIToolkit/WorkIQ style
All deps except fuzzy matching already in pyproject (bs4, backoff, duckduckgo-search core/extras; selenium in agents extra)
No existing company-research capability in parrot — greenfield
Flowtask returns first successful source per company; the right default for an agent tool is undecided
confidence_map.reasoning: behavior read from code; product default is a user decision (U1)

confidence_map.claim: ZoomInfo cannot work HTTP-only (anti-bot); it needs a browser-based fetcher
confidence_map.reasoning: inferred from ZoomInfoScrapper's forced undetected-chrome get(); not re-verified live (U2)

confidence_map.claim: A single toolkit under parrot/tools/company_research/ is the right placement
confidence_map.reasoning: matches conventions; final surface shape pending U3

confidence_map.claim: Selector-coupled parsers may already be stale vs live site markup
confidence_map.reasoning: cannot verify without live requests; treat ported selectors as best-effort

recommended_next_command.rationale: Localization and constraints are high-confidence and the architecture has no real fork (port + adapt onto existing HTTPService/AbstractToolkit); the three unknowns are product defaults resolvable in Q&A, not architectural explorations.

budget_status.note: Q012 stub_credentialed_tool.py skipped — WorkIQTool already covers the credentialed-tool pattern
